testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. Only implementation files changed: CommentThread.vue and pages/[project]/tickets/[ref].vue

criteria.criterion: TicketActionPanel is wired into the right column of pages/[project]/tickets/[ref].vue
criteria.evidence: TicketActionPanel component placed at line 154-158 in right column (col-span-1) with :ticket and :project-slug props

criteria.criterion: CommentThread is wired into the left column below the ticket body
criteria.evidence: CommentThread component placed at line 118-122 in left column (col-span-2), after ticket description

criteria.criterion: A success toast appears after each successful ticket transition
criteria.evidence: onTransition() function (line 76-79) calls toast.success('Ticket updated successfully'), triggered via @transition handler

criteria.criterion: Ticket data refreshes automatically after a transition (useAsyncData refresh())
criteria.evidence: refresh() destructured from useAsyncData (line 30), called in onTransition() at line 78

criteria.criterion: A success toast appears after a comment is added
criteria.evidence: onCommentAdded() function (line 81-83) calls toast.success('Comment added successfully'), triggered via @comment-added handler on CommentThread

criteria.criterion: bun run lint exits 0 with no errors in apps/web
criteria.evidence: ESLint ran successfully with no output (zero errors, zero warnings)

criteria.criterion: bun run type-check exits 0 with no TypeScript errors in apps/web
criteria.evidence: Nuxt typecheck passed with no error TS lines (pre-existing module resolution WARNs are unrelated)

reasoning: All 114 tests pass (19 US-005-4 new tests + 95 existing tests). Implementation is clean and follows conventions. All 7 acceptance criteria are fully met. Code changes are minimal and focused: (1) wire components with proper props and event handlers, (2) add toast/refresh logic in handlers, (3) add comment-added emit to CommentThread. No test files were modified. No security issues. TypeScript types are properly compatible (Ticket interface includes [key: string]: unknown). The implementation is production-ready.
